title: DIY Wooden Halloween Black Cat
summary: This DIY wooden black cat is a fun seasonal woodworking project that you can embellish however you choose!
description: This DIY wooden black cat is a fun seasonal woodworking project that you can embellish however you choose!

steps.title: Trace Cat Shape
steps.body: Free-hand or trace your cat shape onto your project panel. You can find free cat templates online (I designed one in Photoshop using AI which you can access here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1HK3UOXsNqx7LPhm4YOsQKAb3Yu5g9Dsz/view?usp=sharing). You can use the tiling or poster feature in Adobe Acrobat or a similar program and print your template on multiple pages and piece them together to get a larger template. Mine is approximately 18 1/2" x 13". I did modify it when tracing and didn't trace the tail all the way down as it ended up being too flimsy and could break, so keep that in mind.

steps.title: Cut Your Wood
steps.body: Cut your 1x6 base to 14" long (modify depending on the size of your cat), and cut out your cat using a jig saw.

steps.title: Sand
steps.body: Smooth edges and surfaces with a random orbit sander.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Drill 3 pocket holes along the bottom back side of your cat.

steps.title: Attach to Base
steps.body: Center and attach your wooden cat to the base using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Paint and Stain
steps.body: I stained the base with a dark walnut color and painted the cat with an indoor/outdoor black acrylic craft paint.

steps.title: Embellish
steps.body: This is where you can have fun and add unique touches! I added a ribbon collar, and hot glued some fall embellishments onto the base, and gold flat beads for the eyes.
